scope: Phase L: latency hiding for the grid-parallel native decode tile (scheduler now default-on)
scheduler_default: ON (AMD_ISA_SCHED=1 default; banks the +4.6%/+4.0% latency-hiding gain; token_match preserved at both ctx)
long_context_cliff: NONE: native ctx4096 pct (48.1%) >= ctx512 pct (46.4%). FIXED_S -> each workgroup sweeps a constant L=96-token split masked by Tc, so per-workgroup work is ctx-independent. No slope to bend -> cross-iteration modulo scheduling has no long-context target.
latency_vs_occupancy: scheduler gain is small (+4-4.6%) because latency is mostly hidden by occupancy (16 waves/CU at 4 wg/CU). The dominant remaining ~2x lever is OCCUPANCY (Phase M): native LDS=14336 -> 4 wg/CU vs owned LDS=8192 -> 8 wg/CU; owned keeps accumulators in registers, native in LDS (+6144 B).
next_lever: Phase M occupancy (LDS 14336 -> 8192 via register accumulators) -- the real ~2x; latency is occupancy-hidden, not the lever.
